title: PFMW A.3 Large-Font Matrix Cockpit - Post-MVA Lumbar
subtitle: Post-MVA lumbar flexion-intolerant load pattern | Primary marker: forward bend / floor reach
main_marker: Forward bend / reach-to-floor: pain, range, guarding, recovery
lead_card: TX LUM 01 - Lumbar Flexion-Intolerant Load Reset
secondary: Mid-upper back; left knee feels off with ADLs
safety: High-energy MVA. Screen neuro, fracture, knee instability before loading.
page1_note: One-lane rule: Low back leads today. Thoracic and knee are monitored unless safety or marker response changes.

B3.do: Low-force TL / thoracic input if earned

B4.do: Hip hinge to high target + exhale brace

B2.do: Soft tissue only if it opens hinge

B5.do: Light STS or raised reach task

B3.do: Mobility if range still limits work

B7.do: Semi-Stiff Dead Lift OR Suitcase Carry

page2.subtitle: Lead card: TX LUM 01 | Moderate-high irritability | Safety first
safety_gate: Screen lumbar neuro: myotomes, sensation, reflexes if indicated
Ask bowel/bladder, saddle anesthesia, progressive weakness
Knee: swelling, extension loss, true locking, giving way, inability to bear weight
page2.primary_marker: Forward bend / reach-to-floor tolerance. Record pain /10, range, guarding, recovery time, distal symptom behavior. Use raised-object reach if full bend is too provocative.
hep: Diaphragm Breathing: 4-6 slow breaths, 1-2x/day or flare reset
Add Sit-to-Stand or Hip Hinge Chair Squats once stable

protocol_rows.action: Confirm lumbar bending intolerance. Note thoracic and knee as secondary.
protocol_rows.decision: Re-test bend only once. Do not over-test acute MVA.

protocol_rows.action: 90/90 or side-lying unload. Add slow exhale breathing.
protocol_rows.decision: Bend marker should feel less guarded or recover faster.

protocol_rows.action: Brief TL paraspinals / QL / glute if it helps bend. No long passive session.
protocol_rows.decision: If unchanged, stop soft tissue. Do not chase tenderness.

protocol_rows.action: Thoracic or TL impulse/mobilization if restriction links to marker. Avoid aggressive lumbar rotation.
protocol_rows.decision: Keep only if bend improves. No cavitation chasing.

protocol_rows.action: Hip hinge to high target. Cue ribs stacked, exhale, push hips back, reach green zone.
protocol_rows.decision: If better, this is the lead home strategy. If worse, reduce range.

protocol_rows.action: Minimal today. Supported sit-to-stand only if marker calms and knee screen is stable.
protocol_rows.decision: No floor lifting, no >10 lb lifting, no repeated bending until stable.

avoid_today: No stacking extra regions because time is available
secondary_handling: Left knee: screen effusion, extension loss, locking, giving way before loading
Thoracic: supporting doorway only if it improves lumbar bend or breathing
Early knee HEP only if safe: Quad Sets or Heel Slides with Knee Flexion

page3.subtitle: Post-MVA lumbar bending case: calm threat, prove driver, build capacity
expert_read: Post-traumatic lumbar flexion-load sensitivity with guarding and work/lift intolerance
Treat as moderate-high irritability until 24-hour response proves otherwise
Do not let the knee complaint disappear without screening it
retest_logic: Pass: bend farther, less sharp, less guarded, faster recovery, no distal spread
Fail: same/worse after 1-2 logical inputs, more guarding, symptoms move down leg
Decision: pass keeps lane; fail changes dose, marker, or safety screen

lesson_rows.rule: Low back leads today; upper back and knee are secondary screens
lesson_rows.why: Keeps the visit from becoming three mini-plans

lesson_rows.rule: Forward bend / raised reach is the truth test
lesson_rows.why: No marker equals drift. Same marker decides

lesson_rows.rule: Soft tissue, impulse, or mobility must improve bend
lesson_rows.why: Prevents passive dependency and random care

lesson_rows.rule: Smaller range, fewer reps, more support
Raised hinge -> STS -> carries -> floor exposure
lesson_rows.why: Pain relief is not the finish line

page3.patient_script: Your low back is very sensitive to bending load right now from the accident. That does not mean bending is bad forever. Today we are using your forward bend as the scoreboard, calming it, teaching a safer hinge, then rebuilding bending and lifting step by step.
page3.doctor_close: Today we are not trying to do everything. We are improving one marker safely. Your job is small, clean reps and no flare debt. If symptoms travel farther down the leg, weakness worsens, or the knee gives way, stop and tell us.
page3.clinical_bottom_line: Acute post-MVA bending pain is not a license to force mobility or prescribe exercise soup. Clear safety screen, one bend marker, one calming/control lane, immediate re-test, then progressive capacity when 24-hour response is stable.
